items.title: Credit guarantee scheme for MFI 2.0 gets extension
items.summary: The Credit Guarantee Scheme for Microfinance Institutions-2.0 has been extended. This extension will last until August or until guarantees worth ₹20,000 crore are issued. The maximum loan amount for large NBFC-MFIs and MFIs has been raised to ₹1,000 crore. This aims to boost credit flow to the microfinance sector. Loans totaling ₹770 crore have been sanctioned so far.

items.title: No overcapacity in steel, textiles; India's consumption remains low: DGTR
items.summary: India has rejected US claims of excess capacity in its textile and steel sectors. Officials state per capita consumption of these products is very low. India is a net importer of manmade fibres. Trade remedies are vital for fair practices and domestic manufacturing. Many trade defence probes are ongoing.

items.title: India, UAE move to expand strategic crude reserves; Abu Dhabi-linked oil storage may rise to 30 mln barrels
items.summary: India and the UAE are boosting energy security. Plans are underway to significantly expand India's strategic oil reserves with UAE-linked crude storage. New facilities will increase capacity from 5.8 million barrels to 30 million barrels. Both nations are also establishing a framework for strategic gas reserves. This initiative aims to provide greater flexibility and ensure stable energy supplies for India.

items.title: Urea import prices halve in latest tender, offering relief to subsidy bill
items.summary: The urea import tender by National Fertilizers Ltd (NFL) received landed price (cost-plus-freight) quotes in a range of $444.9-449.3 per tonne, two people aware of the development said. This is less than half the levels seen in an earlier procurement, which had drawn bids of $935–959 per tonne.

items.title: RBI proposes unified framework for banks' risk, compliance and audit functions
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has released a draft framework for bank control and assurance functions. This aims to simplify rules and boost governance. The new guidelines will be effective from January 1, 2027. Banks must ensure their risk management, compliance, and internal audit functions are robust.

items.title: Agriculture Ministry's FY27 budget at Rs 1.4 lakh crore a five-fold jump from 2013-14 level
items.summary: The Modi government has five-folded the agriculture ministry budget to Rs 1.4 lakh crore for 2026-27. Over 3,000 climate-resistant crop varieties were released. Farmers received Rs 4.3 lakh crore under PM-KISAN. Fertiliser subsidy rose to Rs 2.21 lakh crore. Foodgrain production reached a record 3,577 lakh tonnes. Agriculture exports grew by 37 percent.

items.title: Over 1 lakh guarantees totalling Rs 48,484 cr issued under ECLG scheme since May 5: FinMin
items.summary: The Emergency Credit Line Guarantee Scheme (ECLGS) 5.0 has seen over 1 lakh guarantees issued, totaling Rs 48,484 crore in just one month. This initiative, aimed at addressing liquidity challenges from the West Asia crisis, primarily benefits the MSME sector, with public sector banks playing a significant role in its rapid adoption.

items.title: RBI allows banks to lend directly to REITs & InvITs, caps apply
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India now permits banks to directly fund Real Estate Investment Trusts and Infrastructure Investment Trusts. Strict rules are in place, including exposure limits and asset eligibility. These changes aim to boost credit access for income-generating trusts while ensuring financial stability. The new framework becomes effective from October 1, 2026.

Govt extends anti-dumping duty on aluminium foil imported from China & 3 other nations till December
items.summary: India has extended anti-dumping duties on aluminium foil imports. The measure will remain in effect until December 15, 2026. This decision impacts imports from China, Malaysia, Thailand, and Indonesia. The Ministry of Finance issued a notification amending previous customs regulations. This action follows requests from domestic companies concerned about unfair trade practices.

items.title: India's outward FDI commitments fall 49% month-on-month to $4.49 bn in May: RBI data
items.summary: India's outward FDI commitments saw a significant 49.02% month-on-month drop to USD 4.49 billion in May 2026, primarily due to reduced equity investments, loans, and guarantees. Despite this decline, total financial commitments by Indian entities overseas increased by 34.6% year-on-year.

items.title: India attracted $843 billion in FDI between 2014-15 and 2025-26: Official
items.summary: India has seen a significant rise in Foreign Direct Investment, attracting USD 843 billion between 2014-15 and 2025-26. Key government initiatives like Make in India and PLI schemes have boosted manufacturing and created numerous jobs. The nation's startup ecosystem is also thriving, with over 2.35 lakh recognized startups generating substantial employment.

items.title: RBI's measures may attract USD 60-70 billion foreign capital, support rupee: India Ratings
items.summary: New measures by the RBI and government aim to attract foreign capital. These steps are expected to bring in USD 60-70 billion. This influx will support the Indian rupee. Reforms also target increased foreign investor participation in government securities. These coordinated efforts strengthen India's financial standing and global market integration.

items.title: Swiggy, Zomato, Uber asked to register gig workers by June 21: Report
items.summary: App-based platforms like Swiggy, Zomato, Uber, Ola, Rapido, Blinkit, and Zepto must register their gig workers on the e-Shram portal by June 21. This directive aims to provide social security benefits, including health insurance and pensions, to millions of workers. The government is creating a comprehensive database to formalize this segment of the economy.
